key_insight: Latent electrostatics can be distilled from foundation ML interatomic potentials using LES, turning fast energy/force predictors into models that also capture charges, long-range electrostatics, and IR response.
engineering_relevance: This enables cheaper atomistic simulations for electrically responsive materials and interfaces, while preserving access to quantities needed for spectroscopy and interfacial electrostatics.
method: Train a lightweight LES-augmented student model on teacher-predicted energies and forces.
Optionally fine-tune the student with additional DFT data for improved accuracy.
Extract Born effective charges and simulate infrared spectra from the distilled model.
evidence_signal: The approach was benchmarked on water, concentrated HCl, and anatase TiO2(101)-water interfaces using students distilled from multiple foundation MLIPs and compared against experimental infrared spectra.
watch_item: Electrostatic and spectroscopic accuracy depends strongly on the teacher’s underlying DFT level and training data, so distillation cannot fully correct a weak or biased foundation model.
paper_title: Distilling latent electrostatics from foundation machine learning interatomic potentials
